title: teucrium polium subsp. aureiforme
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. aureiforme, also known as golden germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium polium plant, a member of the mint family native to the Mediterranean region. This herb has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of golden germander include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and acting as an antiviral agent, while also exhibiting antioxidant properties. Golden germander can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
